name: Grid

values.name: around
values.comment: Pad spacing around items in a row, with space on the left and right hand sides.
values.type: ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | grid : Basics.Never, col : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

values.name: auto
values.comment: Auto column width means that a column expands to fill the available width.

values.name: baseline
values.comment: Aligns items so their balines align at the top of a row.
values.type: ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | grid : Basics.Never, column : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

values.name: between
values.comment: Only pad spacing between items in a row.
values.type: ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | grid : Basics.Never, col : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

values.name: bottom
values.comment: Aligns items at the bottom of a row.
values.type: ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | grid : Basics.Never, column : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

values.name: center
values.comment: Centers a row or column.

values.name: col
values.comment: The column builder for a responsive grid, on which column compatible properties can be defined.
values.type: List.List (List.List (Grid.Grid -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder { a | col : ResponsiveDSL.Compatible } Grid.Grid)) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Html msg) -> Responsive.ResponsiveStyle -> Html.Styled.Html msg

values.name: columns
values.comment:
Defines how many column widths a column will take up. Zero means use 'auto'
width and expand to fill available space.

When applied to a row or grid, this sets the total number of columns available.
When applied to a column, this sets the number of columns taken up out of the
total available.
values.type: Basics.Float -> ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | row : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

values.name: end
values.comment: Puts the content of a row or column at the end.

values.name: first
values.comment: Orders a row or column so it comes first.

values.name: grid
values.comment: The outer builder of a responsive grid.
values.type: List.List (List.List (Grid.Grid -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder { a | grid : ResponsiveDSL.Compatible } Grid.Grid)) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Responsive.ResponsiveStyle -> Html.Styled.Html msg) -> Responsive.ResponsiveStyle -> Html.Styled.Html msg

values.name: last
values.comment: Orders a row or column so it comes last.

values.name: middle
values.comment: Aligns items in the middle of a row.
values.type: ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | grid : Basics.Never, column : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

values.name: offset
values.comment: Defines how many column widths a column is offset from the left hand side by.
values.type: Basics.Float -> ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | grid : Basics.Never, row : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

values.name: reverse
values.comment: Reverses the order of items in a column or row.
values.type: Responsive.Device -> Grid.Grid -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder { a | grid : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

values.name: row
values.comment: The row builder for a responsive grid, on which row compatible properties can be defined.
values.type: List.List (List.List (Grid.Grid -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder { a | row : ResponsiveDSL.Compatible } Grid.Grid)) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Responsive.ResponsiveStyle -> Html.Styled.Html msg) -> Responsive.ResponsiveStyle -> Html.Styled.Html msg

values.name: start
values.comment: Puts the content of a row or column at the start.

values.name: stretch
values.comment: Stretches items to fill the row height-wise.
values.type: ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | grid : Basics.Never, column : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

values.name: top
values.comment: Aligns items at the top of a row.
values.type: ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | grid : Basics.Never, column : Basics.Never } Grid.Grid

name: Responsive

unions.name: Device
unions.comment: Defines the possible classes of device; "small", "medium", "large" or "extra large".

aliases.name: CommonStyle
aliases.comment: Defines the style parameters that are common accross all devices.

aliases.name: DeviceSpec
aliases.comment:
Defines a mapping from devices to something else, that must always include a definition
for each device size.
aliases.type: { sm : a, md : a, lg : a, xl : a }

aliases.name: DeviceStyle
aliases.comment: Defines the style parameters that are device specific.
aliases.type: { device : Responsive.Device, baseFontSize : Basics.Float, breakWidth : Basics.Float, wrapperWidth : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: Mixin
aliases.comment: A mixin is a function that adds styles into a list of styles.

aliases.name: ResponsiveStyle
aliases.comment: Specifies the base styling properties accross all devices.

values.name: deviceStyle
values.comment:
Creates a single CSS property with media queries. Media queries will be
generated for each of the devices specified.

values.name: deviceStyles
values.comment:
Creates a set of CSS properties with media queries. Media queries will be
generated for each of the devices specified.

values.name: fontMediaStyles
values.comment:
Creates font-size and line-height accross all media devices using media queries,
for a supplied font size level. These font sizings will be in keeping with the
vertical rhythm.

values.name: fontSizeMixin
values.comment:
A mixin that for a given type scale and font size level, creates font-size
and line-height properties in keeping with the vertical rhythm.

values.name: global
values.comment:
A globaal CSS style sheet that sets up basic spaing for text, with single
direction margins.

values.name: lineHeight
values.comment: Calculates the line height for a base styling.

values.name: mapMaybeDeviceSpec
values.comment:
Maps a device spec with optional values into a list, where the list only contains values
for the device specs that were actually defined.

values.name: mapMixins
values.comment:
Applies a list of mixins to a set of CSS properties, to produce a new list of CSS properties
with all the mixins applied.

TODO: Is this right? Looks like each mixin is being added into each style, than all are being
concatentated together - which ought to lead to duplicates? I think perhaps the mixins should be
chained together, not applied single then concatenated.
values.type: List.List Responsive.Mixin -> List.List Css.Style -> List.List Css.Style

values.name: mediaMixins
values.comment:
Given a set of devices, and a function to build mixins from the device properties,
creates a list of mixins, one for each device type.

The smallest (sm) device is applied without a media query, and the larger
sizes are successively applied with media queries on their break widths.

In this way, a mixin that is dependant on device properties can be applied accross
all device. Use `mapMixins` to apply the list of mixins over a list of base styles.

values.name: rhythm
values.comment:
Calculates a multiple of the line height for a base styling.

This produces a result in px, which works the most accurately.

values.name: rhythmEm
values.comment:
Calculates a multiple of the line height for a base styling.

This produces a result in em, which is not as accurate as px. Sometimes
expressing in em is easier, as that adapts.

values.name: styleAsMixin
values.comment: Turns a single CSS property into a mixin.

name: Styles

values.name: empty
values.comment: An empty style, for convenience when sketching out DSLs.

values.name: lg
values.comment: Large device grid property builder.
values.type: List.List (Responsive.Device -> ctx -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder a ctx) -> List.List (ctx -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder a ctx)

values.name: md
values.comment: Medium device grid property builder.
values.type: List.List (Responsive.Device -> ctx -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder a ctx) -> List.List (ctx -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder a ctx)

values.name: sm
values.comment: Small device grid property builder.
values.type: List.List (Responsive.Device -> ctx -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder a ctx) -> List.List (ctx -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder a ctx)

values.name: styles
values.comment: Adds any CSS style you like to a grid element.
values.type: List.List Css.Style -> Responsive.Device -> ctx -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder a ctx

values.name: xl
values.comment: Extra large device grid property builder.
values.type: List.List (Responsive.Device -> ctx -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder a ctx) -> List.List (ctx -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder a ctx)

name: TheSett.Buttons

values.name: button
values.comment: Creates a button.
values.type: List.List (List.List (TheSett.Buttons.Button -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder { a | button : ResponsiveDSL.Compatible } TheSett.Buttons.Button)) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Html msg) -> Responsive.ResponsiveStyle -> Html.Styled.Html msg

name: TheSett.Cards

values.name: body
values.comment: Defines the body of the card.

values.name: card
values.comment: Creates a Card container.
values.type: List.List (List.List (TheSett.Cards.Card -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder { a | card : ResponsiveDSL.Compatible } TheSett.Cards.Card)) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Responsive.ResponsiveStyle -> Html.Styled.Html msg) -> Responsive.ResponsiveStyle -> Html.Styled.Html msg

values.name: controls
values.comment: Defines the controls on the card

values.name: height
values.comment: Sets the height of the image, in rhythm units.
values.type: Basics.Float -> Responsive.Device -> TheSett.Cards.Card -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder { a | card : Basics.Never } TheSett.Cards.Card

values.name: image
values.comment: Creates an image on the card.
values.type: List.List (List.List (TheSett.Cards.Card -> ResponsiveDSL.Builder { a | image : ResponsiveDSL.Compatible } TheSett.Cards.Card)) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Html msg) -> Responsive.ResponsiveStyle -> Html.Styled.Html msg

values.name: src
values.comment: Sets the URL of the image.
values.type: String.String -> ResponsiveDSL.StyleBuilder { a | card : Basics.Never } TheSett.Cards.Card

values.name: title
values.comment: Creates a title on the card.

name: TheSett.Debug
comment:
DebugStyle provides a debug stylesheet.

This stylesheet uses repeating background image functions in CSS to display borders
around different elements of a page in different colors, without chaging the size they
are rendered at (as setting a border 1px would do).

The vertical rhtythm is also drawn in as fitn background lines.


# The CSS snippet to add to the global style.

@docs global

values.name: global
values.comment: The debug CSS.

name: TheSett.Laf
comment:
The Sett Look and Feel

@docs style, fonts, responsiveMeta, devices, wrapper

values.name: fonts
values.comment: Links for loading fonts.

values.name: responsiveMeta
values.comment: Meta tag to include to indiciate that the layout is reponsive.

values.name: style
values.comment: The CSS as an HTML <style> element.

values.name: wrapper
values.comment: A responsive wrapper div.

name: TheSett.Logo
comment:
The Sett logo as SVG.

@docs logo

values.name: logo

name: TheSett.Reset
comment:
Provides a standard CSS reset stylesheet, aimed at getting consitent
behaviour accross browsers.


# The CSS snippet to add to the global style.

@docs global

values.name: global
values.comment: The CSS reset.

name: TypeScale

unions.name: FontSizeLevel
unions.comment:
Describes font sizes as a 'level'. The level is the number of steps from the base
level at 1. A minimum number of rhythm lines is also specified, to aid vertical spacing.

aliases.name: TypeScale
aliases.comment: A function that maps a font 'level' to a scaling from the default size.

values.name: augmentedFourth
values.comment: The augmented fourth ratio.

values.name: base
values.comment: The base level

values.name: goldenRatio
values.comment: The golden ratio.

values.name: h1
values.comment: The h1 level

values.name: h2
values.comment: The h2 level

values.name: h3
values.comment: The h3 level

values.name: h4
values.comment: The h4 level

values.name: majorSecond
values.comment: The major second ratio.

values.name: majorThird
values.comment: The major third ratio.

values.name: milli
values.comment: A level down from the base level.

values.name: minorSecond
values.comment: The minor second ratio.

values.name: minorThird
values.comment: The minor third ratio.

values.name: perfectFifth
values.comment: The perfect fifth ratio.

values.name: perfectFourth
values.comment: The perfect fourth ratio.

values.name: typeScale
values.comment: Build a TypeScale function from a ratio between font levels.
